summary.intent: The user invoked a 'last30days' skill to research a topic across multiple sources (Reddit, X, YouTube, Hacker News, GitHub), but no topic was provided.
summary.outcome: The assistant prompted the user for a topic before proceeding; no research was actually conducted. Several frontend files were modified, likely unrelated to the skill invocation.

repo: The project has a Rust web crate (crates/web) with a UI layer built in TypeScript/React (login-app.tsx, onboarding-view.js)
Frontend assets are compiled/bundled into crates/web/src/assets/dist/ with chunked output
There is an onboarding flow (onboarding.js, onboarding-view.js) and a login flow (login.js, login-app.tsx)

code.finding: Login UI is implemented as a React TSX component
Event handling logic lives in a non-bundled JS file separate from the dist output
WebSocket connection logic is split into its own chunk
Voice utility logic is bundled as a separate chunk, suggesting voice features in the app

workflow: The 'last30days' skill requires a topic argument and will prompt if none is provided before executing its pipeline
The skill pipeline is: plan → retrieve → normalize → fuse → rerank → cluster → render

friction: The skill was invoked without a required topic argument, causing the pipeline to stall at the prompt stage

The transcript does not clarify why frontend files were modified during what appears to be a research/skill invocation session — the connection between the file changes and the user's intent is unclear
open_items: The user never provided a topic, so the last30days research pipeline was never executed
The purpose of the frontend file modifications (login, onboarding, voice, WebSocket) is not captured in the transcript and may represent unrelated in-progress work
